overview: Accelerate into massive mana by casting cheap noncreature spells, leveraging Kinnan’s self-damage to fuel a high-impact creature finisher. Wins by out-resourcing midrange and combo decks, and racing aggressive token or ramp strategies before they stabilize.
win_conditions: Deploy high-impact creatures like Krenko, Mob Boss or similar token generators when you have 10+ mana.
Use remaining mana to pump tokens, cast additional spells, or swing for lethal.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2–3 lands (including basics for Kinnan’s damage or fetchable sources), at least one mana accelerator (Sol Ring, Llanowar Elves, or similar), and Kinnan.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan hands with 0–1 lands, multiple copies of the same ramp spell, or no way to cast Kinnan by turn 2–3.

early_game: Turn 1: Play land, cast Sol Ring or Llanowar Elves.
Turn 2: Play land, cast Kinnan (or another ramp spell if Kinnan is in hand).
Sequence lands and noncreature spells to maximize mana output while monitoring life total.
Prioritize casting cheap cantrips and mana rocks to build your engine before deploying threats.
mid_game: Balance mana for casting noncreature spells versus holding up for your finisher.
Avoid over-committing to cantrips if you cannot follow up with a win condition.
Manage life total carefully, as Kinnan’s damage accumulates quickly.
Commit threats only when you have sufficient mana to cast your finisher and a plan to protect it or follow up.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers, heavy interaction, or a clear path to stabilization.
late_game: Deploy high-impact creatures like Krenko, Mob Boss or similar token generators when you have 10+ mana.
Use remaining mana to pump tokens, cast additional spells, or swing for lethal.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to casting your finisher, the opponent has no sweepers, and you can win before they stabilize or counter your plan.

sequencing_tips: Common mistakes include forgetting Kinnan’s damage, over-casting noncreature spells without a finisher, and mismanaging life total.
Unique interactions: Kinnan’s damage can be mitigated with early life gain or damage prevention.
Synergizes heavily with cheap cantrips and mana rocks.
Always track opponent interaction to time your finisher.

matchups.advice: Use cheap, multiple spells to beat counters, or cast your finisher when the opponent has no mana up. Utilize protection, evasion, or split spells if available.
Race them by deploying threats earlier. Do not get bogged down in a mana race; use removal on their key ramp pieces and pressure their life total.
Stabilize with early removal or life gain, then pivot to your game plan. Avoid overextending into combat and focus on shutting down their early threats.
Pressure with early threats and use cantrips to dig past counters. Win before they can set up their late-game plan or find their sweepers.
Win by out-resourcing, having better interaction, or deploying your finisher first. Track each other’s noncreature spell counts, life totals, and interaction to time your plays correctly.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), counters, and targeted removal.
Note when opponents play cheap interaction versus late-game answers, and track life totals to avoid dying to Kinnan’s damage or combat.
